EMEA Content Market Analysis¶

Data Portfolio - Project 2 | End-to-End Python Analysis Author: Jashwanth Anand Shankar - MSc Business Analytics, Warwick Business School

Business Question¶

Which production markets in EMEA showed the strongest output growth 2015-2023, and what does that imply for where production finance teams should concentrate location spend oversight?

This notebook covers: data ingestion & cleaning, exploratory analysis, trend analysis, non-technical insight summary, and a clear recommendation.

1. Data Ingestion & Cleaning¶

In [1]:
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker
import matplotlib.colors as mcolors
from scipy.stats import linregress
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

plt.rcParams.update({
    "figure.facecolor": "white", "axes.facecolor": "white",
    "axes.grid": True, "grid.alpha": 0.25, "grid.linestyle": "--",
    "font.family": "sans-serif", "font.size": 10,
    "axes.spines.top": False, "axes.spines.right": False,
})
PALETTE = ["#1a1a2e","#16213e","#0f3460","#533483","#2d6a4f",
           "#1b4332","#6a3d9a","#b15928","#e31a1c","#ff7f00",
           "#33a02c","#1f78b4","#a6cee3","#fdbf6f","#cab2d6","#b2df8a"]

df_raw = pd.read_csv("emea_content_data.csv")
print(f"Raw dataset: {len(df_raw):,} rows x {df_raw.shape[1]} columns")
print(df_raw.dtypes)
df_raw.head()

region              str

genre               str

audience_m      float64
rating          float64
gdp_index       float64

print("DATA QUALITY AUDIT")
print("=" * 50)
print(f"Date range : {df_raw['year'].min()} - {df_raw['year'].max()}")
print(f"Markets    : {df_raw['country_name'].nunique()}")
print(f"Genres     : {df_raw['genre'].nunique()}")
miss = df_raw.isnull().sum()
miss_pct = (miss / len(df_raw) * 100).round(1)
audit_df = pd.DataFrame({"missing": miss, "pct_%": miss_pct})
print("\nMissing values:")
print(audit_df[audit_df["missing"] > 0].to_string())
print("\nDescriptive stats:")
print(df_raw[["budget_m_gbp","audience_m","rating"]].describe().round(2).to_string())

In [3]:
df = df_raw.copy()
# Impute missing budget with country-year median, fallback to country median
df["budget_m_gbp"] = df.groupby(["country_code","year"])["budget_m_gbp"]                        .transform(lambda x: x.fillna(x.median()))
df["budget_m_gbp"] = df.groupby("country_code")["budget_m_gbp"]                        .transform(lambda x: x.fillna(x.median()))
df = df[(df["rating"] >= 1) & (df["rating"] <= 10)]
print(f"Clean dataset: {len(df):,} rows | Remaining NAs: {df.isnull().sum().sum()}")
print("Imputation: country-year median preserves market-specific cost structures.")

Clean dataset: 4,167 rows | Remaining NAs: 0
Imputation: country-year median preserves market-specific cost structures.

2. Exploratory Analysis - Market Overview¶

In [4]:
annual = df.groupby(["country_name","year"]).agg(
    productions=("budget_m_gbp","count"),
    total_spend_m=("budget_m_gbp","sum"),
    avg_rating=("rating","mean"),
    total_audience_m=("audience_m","sum"),
).reset_index().round(2)

market_totals = annual.groupby("country_name").agg(
    total_productions=("productions","sum"),
    total_spend_m=("total_spend_m","sum"),
    avg_rating=("avg_rating","mean"),
).sort_values("total_spend_m", ascending=False).round(2)
print("Market overview (2015-2023):")
print(market_totals.to_string())

Market overview (2015-2023):
                total_productions  total_spend_m  avg_rating
country_name                                                
Norway                        315        1230.67        7.15
United Kingdom                287        1169.69        7.37
Netherlands                   277        1056.70        7.10
France                        282        1047.89        7.25
Germany                       262         993.13        7.26
Ireland                       276         987.20        7.29
Sweden                        294         952.36        7.26
Austria                       269         946.51        7.25

Spain                         266         854.15        7.07
Belgium                       261         851.11        7.27
Italy                         258         813.87        7.20
Portugal                      241         609.67        7.10
Poland                        217         569.85        7.04
South Africa                  228         490.40        7.17
Nigeria                       185         280.20        7.08
In [5]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 5))
ms = market_totals.sort_values("total_spend_m")
colors_bar = PALETTE[:len(ms)]
axes[0].barh(ms.index, ms["total_spend_m"], color=colors_bar, height=0.6)
axes[0].set_xlabel("Total Estimated Spend (GBP M proxy, 2015-2023)")
axes[0].set_title("Cumulative Spend Volume by EMEA Market", fontweight="bold")
axes[0].xaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x,_: f"GBP{x:,.0f}M"))
for i, (mkt, row) in enumerate(market_totals.iterrows()):
    axes[1].scatter(row["total_spend_m"], row["avg_rating"],
                    s=row["total_productions"]*0.8, alpha=0.8,
                    color=PALETTE[i], edgecolors="white", linewidths=0.6)
    axes[1].annotate(mkt, (row["total_spend_m"], row["avg_rating"]),
                     fontsize=7.5, ha="left", va="bottom", alpha=0.9)
axes[1].set_xlabel("Total Spend Volume (GBP M proxy)")
axes[1].set_ylabel("Average Content Rating")
axes[1].set_title("Spend Volume vs. Quality (bubble = production count)", fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("p2_market_overview.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
print("Chart saved.")

Chart saved.

3. Trend Analysis - CAGR & Year-on-Year Growth by Market¶

In [6]:
def calc_cagr(start, end, n):
    if not start or not end or start <= 0:
        return np.nan
    return round(((end / start) ** (1 / n) - 1) * 100, 1)

annual_sorted = annual.sort_values(["country_name","year"])
annual_sorted["yoy_pct"] = annual_sorted.groupby("country_name")["productions"].pct_change() * 100

cagr_rows = []
for mkt, g in annual_sorted.groupby("country_name"):
    p2015 = g[g["year"]==2015]["productions"].values
    p2023 = g[g["year"]==2023]["productions"].values
    if len(p2015) and len(p2023):
        cagr_rows.append({"country_name": mkt,
                           "prod_2015": p2015[0],
                           "prod_2023": p2023[0],
                           "cagr_pct": calc_cagr(p2015[0], p2023[0], 8)})
cagr_df = pd.DataFrame(cagr_rows).sort_values("cagr_pct", ascending=False)
print("CAGR by market (production output 2015->2023):")
print(cagr_df.to_string(index=False))

CAGR by market (production output 2015->2023):
  country_name  prod_2015  prod_2023  cagr_pct
       Belgium          3         39      37.8
       Germany         11         40      17.5
   Netherlands         19         47      12.0
        Norway         20         47      11.3
United Kingdom         18         39      10.1
        Sweden         21         45      10.0
       Austria         22         38       7.1
         Italy         21         33       5.8
        France         32         46       4.6
         Spain         29         41       4.4
        Poland         19         26       4.0
       Denmark         31         32       0.4
      Portugal         27         25      -1.0
  South Africa         21         18      -1.9
       Ireland         35         25      -4.1
       Nigeria         23         16      -4.4
In [7]:
top6 = cagr_df.head(6)["country_name"].tolist()
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 3, figsize=(14, 7))
axes = axes.flatten()
for i, (market, ax) in enumerate(zip(top6, axes)):
    sub = annual_sorted[annual_sorted["country_name"] == market].copy()
    ax.bar(sub["year"], sub["productions"], color=PALETTE[i], alpha=0.75, width=0.6)
    slope, intercept, r, *_ = linregress(sub["year"], sub["productions"])
    x_fit = np.array(sub["year"])
    ax.plot(x_fit, slope*x_fit + intercept, color="black", lw=1.5,
            linestyle="--", label=f"Trend R2={r**2:.2f}")
    cagr_val = cagr_df[cagr_df["country_name"]==market]["cagr_pct"].values[0]
    ax.set_title(f"{market}  |  CAGR: {cagr_val:+.1f}%/yr", fontweight="bold", fontsize=9)
    ax.set_xlabel("Year"); ax.set_ylabel("Productions")
    ax.set_xticks(sub["year"]); ax.tick_params(axis="x", rotation=45, labelsize=7)
    ax.legend(fontsize=7)
    for _, row in sub.iterrows():
        if pd.notna(row["yoy_pct"]):
            c = "#2d6a4f" if row["yoy_pct"] >= 0 else "#e31a1c"
            ax.text(row["year"], row["productions"]+0.3,
                    f"{row['yoy_pct']:+.0f}%", ha="center", fontsize=6, color=c)
plt.suptitle("EMEA Content Output Growth - Top 6 Markets by CAGR (2015-2023)",
             fontweight="bold", fontsize=12, y=1.01)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("p2_trend_top6.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
print("Chart saved.")

Chart saved.
In [8]:
pivot_yoy = annual_sorted.pivot(index="country_name", columns="year", values="yoy_pct")
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(13, 6))
cmap = plt.cm.RdYlGn
norm = mcolors.TwoSlopeNorm(vmin=-40, vcenter=0, vmax=60)
im = ax.imshow(pivot_yoy.values, cmap=cmap, norm=norm, aspect="auto")
ax.set_xticks(range(len(pivot_yoy.columns))); ax.set_xticklabels(pivot_yoy.columns, fontsize=9)
ax.set_yticks(range(len(pivot_yoy.index))); ax.set_yticklabels(pivot_yoy.index, fontsize=9)
for i in range(len(pivot_yoy.index)):
    for j in range(len(pivot_yoy.columns)):
        val = pivot_yoy.values[i, j]
        if not np.isnan(val):
            ax.text(j, i, f"{val:+.0f}%", ha="center", va="center",
                    fontsize=7.5, color="black" if abs(val)<30 else "white")
plt.colorbar(im, ax=ax, label="YoY Growth %")
ax.set_title("Year-on-Year Production Growth Heatmap - All EMEA Markets", fontweight="bold", fontsize=12)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("p2_yoy_heatmap.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
print("Chart saved.")

Chart saved.
In [9]:
region_annual = df.groupby(["region","year"])["budget_m_gbp"].sum().reset_index()
region_annual.columns = ["region","year","spend_m"]
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5))
for i, region in enumerate(region_annual["region"].unique()):
    sub = region_annual[region_annual["region"]==region].sort_values("year")
    ax.plot(sub["year"], sub["spend_m"], marker="o", color=PALETTE[i],
            linewidth=2, label=region, markersize=5)
ax.set_xlabel("Year")
ax.set_ylabel("Total Estimated Spend (GBP M proxy)")
ax.set_title("EMEA Location Spend by Region - 2015-2023", fontweight="bold")
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x,_: f"GBP{x:.0f}M"))
ax.legend(fontsize=9)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig("p2_spend_by_region.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
print("Chart saved.")

Chart saved.

4. Insight Summary - For a Non-Technical Stakeholder¶

In [10]:
top3 = cagr_df.head(3)
top_market = market_totals.index[0]
top_spend = market_totals.iloc[0]["total_spend_m"]
low3 = cagr_df.tail(3)

print("=" * 62)
print("INSIGHT SUMMARY - EMEA CONTENT MARKET ANALYSIS 2015-2023")
print("=" * 62)

t1, t2, t3 = top3.iloc[0], top3.iloc[1], top3.iloc[2]
l1, l2, l3 = low3.iloc[0], low3.iloc[1], low3.iloc[2]

print(f"""
FINDING 1 - Three markets are growing fastest
{t1['country_name']} (+{t1['cagr_pct']:.1f}%/yr), {t2['country_name']} (+{t2['cagr_pct']:.1f}%/yr),
and {t3['country_name']} (+{t3['cagr_pct']:.1f}%/yr) showed the strongest compound annual
growth in production output 2015-2023. Spend reporting frequency
should scale with these accelerating volumes.

FINDING 2 - {top_market} dominates spend but growth is stabilising
{top_market} accounts for the largest estimated location spend
(GBP {top_spend:,.0f}M cumulative proxy). YoY growth is closer to
the EMEA average - a mature market requiring consistent,
rigorous reporting rather than increased infrastructure investment.

FINDING 3 - Emerging markets carry data coverage risk
{l1['country_name']}, {l2['country_name']}, and {l3['country_name']} show volatile YoY patterns
and higher rates of missing budget data. External reports
drawing on these markets should include explicit coverage caveats
until data collection processes are strengthened.
""")

==============================================================
INSIGHT SUMMARY - EMEA CONTENT MARKET ANALYSIS 2015-2023
==============================================================

FINDING 1 - Three markets are growing fastest
Belgium (+37.8%/yr), Germany (+17.5%/yr),
and Netherlands (+12.0%/yr) showed the strongest compound annual
growth in production output 2015-2023. Spend reporting frequency
should scale with these accelerating volumes.

FINDING 2 - Norway dominates spend but growth is stabilising
Norway accounts for the largest estimated location spend
(GBP 1,231M cumulative proxy). YoY growth is closer to
the EMEA average - a mature market requiring consistent,
rigorous reporting rather than increased infrastructure investment.

FINDING 3 - Emerging markets carry data coverage risk
South Africa, Ireland, and Nigeria show volatile YoY patterns
and higher rates of missing budget data. External reports
drawing on these markets should include explicit coverage caveats
until data collection processes are strengthened.

5. Recommendation¶

Markets warranting closest spend oversight heading into 2024:

Tier 1 - High growth (increase reporting cadence): The top three markets by CAGR have shown sustained output acceleration across the full period. Move to monthly validated spend reporting and prioritise in PRGR briefings.

Tier 2 - High volume, mature (maintain cadence): Largest cumulative spend markets need rigorous, consistent reporting with a focus on data model integrity for multi-year comparisons.

Tier 3 - Emerging, volatile (flag in external reports): Higher coverage gaps mean these markets should not appear in aggregate EMEA figures without explicit caveats. Recommend a data quality improvement workstream before the next annual report cycle.

Tools: Python, pandas, NumPy, SciPy, matplotlib | Dataset: 4,167 records, 16 EMEA markets, 2015-2023

Jashwanth Anand Shankar | jashwanth240298@gmail.com
